summary: Do not ship. The committed RED record matches `red.sha`, its tree, and the exact DATA LOSS assertion, but P-4's T-P4b witness is red for the wrong reason and does not exercise settlement. No other new critical P-4 issue found. Simpler alternative: none; repair the test choreography without changing the fix model. Open question: none.

findings.title: T-P4b lands the pointer before the reference snapshot
findings.body: Steps 2–4 establish that the metadata rename and `landed` insertion completed before starting `run_once_at`. The plan's own execution order collects references before processing blobs, so that snapshot will include the now-visible pointer. Reconciliation therefore takes the referenced branch and never calls `grave()` or `settle()`. The expected restore log will be absent, making T-P4b red because its setup bypasses the target seam; without that assertion it could remain green even if landed-first restoration were removed. This proves neither immediate restoration nor the no-404 P-4 seal.
findings.recommendation: Start reconciliation first and park it at `pre_grave`, after `collect_referenced` has missed the pointer. Then advance the put through pointer rename and park it at `in_commit_post_landed`; release `pre_grave` and assert that settlement restores immediately while the pin remains live. If landed notification remains load-bearing, add an ordering where settlement is already waiting when the rename lands so removing `notify_waiters()` is deterministically RED.

next_steps: Correct T-P4b's ordering and assertions without expanding beyond P-4.
Run the approved scoped plan re-review again; leave machine-owned regression and GREEN execution untouched.
